changes.value:
The frontend has hardcoded TypeScript interfaces (Task, Tag, Column, Board) that mirror entity fields as typed properties. This is fundamentally wrong — entities are dynamic bags of fields defined by the schema. Any TS interface will get out of sync when fields are added/renamed/removed.

## Current problem
- `types/kanban.ts` defines `Task { id, title, description, position, tags, ... }`
- Components read `task.title`, `task.description` as typed properties
- Update calls hardcode field names: `field_name: "title"`, `field_name: "body"`
- None of this consults the field registry
- Custom user fields are invisible to the UI

## What should happen
- The frontend works with `Entity` (entity_type + id + fields: Record<string, unknown>)
- The frontend fetches `EntitySchema` (entity def + field defs) via `get_entity_schema`
- Components render fields by iterating FieldDef[], not by accessing typed properties
- Field name for updates comes from `fieldDef.name`, not a string literal
- Display/editor type comes from `fieldDef.display`/`fieldDef.editor`
- This is the foundation for Phase 3 (Grid) column generation from field registry

## Scope
- Remove or deprecate Task, Tag, Column typed interfaces
- BoardView, TaskCard, TaskDetailPanel, TagInspector render from Entity + FieldDef[]
- get_board and list_tasks return entities with field bags, not typed objects
- Update calls use fieldDef.name from schema
- The card title is just the field where fieldDef.name matches the entity's "title-like" field

## This blocks
- Phase 3 Grid (column generation from field registry)
- Custom user fields
- Field rename/add/delete in the UI
- Correct undo/redo (field names must come from schema)
Replace typed Task/Tag/Column interfaces with schema-driven Entity rendering

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,29 +1,47 @@
-The frontend has hardcoded TypeScript interfaces (Task, Tag, Column, Board) that mirror entity fields as typed properties. This is fundamentally wrong — entities are dynamic bags of fields defined by the schema. Any TS interface will get out of sync when fields are added/renamed/removed.
+The frontend has hardcoded TypeScript interfaces (Task, Tag, Column, Board) that mirror entity fields as typed properties. Entities are dynamic bags of fields defined by the schema — the UI should use `entity_type` as the discriminant and FieldDef[] for rendering, not OO types.
+
+## Design
+
+One type for all entities:
+```typescript
+interface Entity {
+  entity_type: string  // 'task', 'tag', 'column', 'board', etc.
+  id: string
+  fields: Record<string, unknown>
+}
+```
+
+The UI knows what KIND of entity it's showing (entity_type = 'task' for board cards, 'column' for lanes, etc.) but accesses field values through the fields bag, not typed properties. Field metadata (name, editor, display, sort) comes from `get_entity_schema(entity_type)`.
+
+## What changes
+
+### Remove
+- `interface Task { id, title, description, position, tags, ... }` 
+- `interface Tag { id, name, color, ... }`
+- `interface Column { id, name, order, ... }`
+- All typed property access like `task.title`, `tag.color`, `column.name`

-## Current problem
-- `types/kanban.ts` defines `Task { id, title, description, position, tags, ... }`
-- Components read `task.title`, `task.description` as typed properties
-- Update calls hardcode field names: `field_name: "title"`, `field_name: "body"`
-- None of this consults the field registry
-- Custom user fields are invisible to the UI
+### Keep
+- `entity_type` as the string discriminant — the board knows it renders 'task' entities as cards and 'column' entities as lanes
+- `FieldDef`, `EntityDef`, `EntitySchema` — the schema types that describe what fields an entity type has
+- `Entity` as the single generic type
+
+### Field access pattern
+```typescript
+// Instead of: task.title
+entity.fields[titleFieldDef.name]

-## What should happen
-- The frontend works with `Entity` (entity_type + id + fields: Record<string, unknown>)
-- The frontend fetches `EntitySchema` (entity def + field defs) via `get_entity_schema`
-- Components render fields by iterating FieldDef[], not by accessing typed properties
-- Field name for updates comes from `fieldDef.name`, not a string literal
-- Display/editor type comes from `fieldDef.display`/`fieldDef.editor`
-- This is the foundation for Phase 3 (Grid) column generation from field registry
+// Instead of: field_name: "title" 
+field_name: fieldDef.name
+```

-## Scope
-- Remove or deprecate Task, Tag, Column typed interfaces
-- BoardView, TaskCard, TaskDetailPanel, TagInspector render from Entity + FieldDef[]
-- get_board and list_tasks return entities with field bags, not typed objects
-- Update calls use fieldDef.name from schema
-- The card title is just the field where fieldDef.name matches the entity's "title-like" field
+### Schema loading
+- App loads entity schemas on startup via `get_entity_schema`
+- Schema context provides field defs to all components
+- Components look up field defs by entity_type, not by hardcoded names

 ## This blocks
 - Phase 3 Grid (column generation from field registry)
 - Custom user fields
 - Field rename/add/delete in the UI
-- Correct undo/redo (field names must come from schema)
\ No newline at end of file
+- Correct field-level undo/redo"
\ No newline at end of file

+The frontend has hardcoded TypeScript interfaces (Task, Tag, Column, Board) that mirror entity fields as typed properties. This is fundamentally wrong — entities are dynamic bags of fields defined by the schema. Any TS interface will get out of sync when fields are added/renamed/removed.

-

-

-- `interface Tag { id, name, color, ... }`

+## What should happen

-### Field access pattern
-```typescript
-// Instead of: task.title
-entity.fields[titleFieldDef.name]
-
-// Instead of: field_name: "title" 
-field_name: fieldDef.name
-```

+- BoardView, TaskCard, TaskDetailPanel, TagInspector render from Entity + FieldDef[]
+- get_board and list_tasks return entities with field bags, not typed objects
+- Update calls use fieldDef.name from schema
+- The card title is just the field where fieldDef.name matches the entity's "title-like" field

-### Schema loading
-- App loads entity schemas on startup via `get_entity_schema`
-- Schema context provides field defs to all components
-- Components look up field defs by entity_type, not by hardcoded names
-

 - Phase 3 Grid (column generation from field registry)
 - Custom user fields
 - Field rename/add/delete in the UI
-- Correct field-level undo/redo"
\ No newline at end of file
+- Correct undo/redo (field names must come from schema)
\ No newline at end of file
